publication.showAs: Official Journal of the European Union, L 325/1, 16 December 2019
publication.name: oj

eventRef.showAs: European Parliament first reading position adopted in plenary
Council of the EU formal adoption
Signed by the Presidents of the EP and Council (mapped to #promulgation per EU convention; see AI Act researchNote 8)
Published in the Official Journal of the European Union (OJ L 325, p. 1)
Entry into force (20 days after OJ publication)
Amended by Reg (EU) 2024/1689 (AI Act) Art 109 — adds requirement to take into account AI Act Chapter III Section 2 when adopting Art 11(2) implementing acts on AI safety components

TLCOrganization.showAs: European Parliament and Council of the European Union (co-legislators)

proprietary.akndiff:actStatus: in force (general application from 6 July 2022; subsequently amended by Reg 2024/1689 Art 109)
proprietary.akndiff:issuingBody: European Parliament and Council of the European Union
proprietary.akndiff:researchNotes:
STUB. Modeled minimally to resolve the AI Act's two outgoing references to /akn/eu/act/2019-11-27/reg-2019-2144 (one in the long title, one in Article 109 amending clause).
Same role as ley-27-1999 plays for Spanish Ley 1/2026 — the prior act that the new modifying act amends. Spanish modeling kept ley-27-1999 fully structural (120 articles as shells) because the ES experiment was specifically about modifying-law dynamics. For EU we kept this stub minimal because the substantive content of the prior act (motor vehicle safety) is not what the AI Act experiment is testing; the schema friction being captured is the cross-act amending pattern (AI Act researchNote 2 confirms this is cross-country).
No new EU-specific frictions surfaced from this stub that aren't already in the AI Act researchNotes.

longTitle.p: REGULATION (EU) 2019/2144 OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL of 27 November 2019 on type-approval requirements for motor vehicles and their trailers, and systems, components and separate technical units intended for such vehicles, as regards their general safety and the protection of vehicle occupants and vulnerable road users, amending Regulation (EU) 2018/858 of the European Parliament and of the Council and repealing Regulations (EC) No 78/2009, (EC) No 79/2009 and (EC) No 661/2009 of the European Parliament and of the Council and Commission Regulations (EC) No 631/2009, (EU) No 406/2010, (EU) No 672/2010, (EU) No 1003/2010, (EU) No 1005/2010, (EU) No 1008/2010, (EU) No 1009/2010, (EU) No 19/2011, (EU) No 109/2011, (EU) No 458/2011, (EU) No 65/2012, (EU) No 130/2012, (EU) No 347/2012, (EU) No 351/2012, (EU) No 1230/2012 and (EU) 2015/166 (Text with EEA relevance).

preamble.p: THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND THE COUNCIL OF THE EUROPEAN UNION, Having regard to the Treaty on the Functioning of the European Union, and in particular Article 114 thereof, [...] Whereas: [38 recitals on road safety policy, vulnerable road users, advanced driver-assistance systems, automated vehicles, repeal of older fragmented type-approval acts ...] HAVE ADOPTED THIS REGULATION:

content.p: Estructura preservada. Texto íntegro en CELEX:32019R2144.

article.heading: Advanced vehicle systems for all motor vehicle categories

content.p: Estructura preservada — intelligent speed assistance, alcohol interlock interface, drowsiness warning, distraction warning, emergency stop signal, reversing detection, event data recorder.

article.heading: Specific requirements relating to passenger cars and light commercial vehicles
Specific requirements relating to passenger cars and light commercial vehicles concerning protection of vulnerable road users
Specific requirements relating to buses, coaches and heavy commercial vehicles
Specific requirements concerning automated vehicles and fully automated vehicles

content.p: Estructura preservada — type-approval framework for automated and fully automated vehicles, the most directly AI-relevant article (later affected by AI Act Art 109 amendment).

article.heading: Type-approval procedures and obligations of approval authorities

content.p: Estructura preservada. Para 3 added by AI Act (Reg 2024/1689) Art 109: "When adopting the implementing acts pursuant to paragraph 2, concerning artificial intelligence systems which are safety components within the meaning of Regulation (EU) 2024/1689, the requirements set out in Chapter III, Section 2, of that Regulation shall be taken into account."
Entry into force: 5 January 2020 (20 days after OJ publication). General application: 6 July 2022.

hcontainer.name: annex
hcontainer.heading: List of requirements referred to in Article 4(5) and Article 5(1)

hcontainer.name: annex
hcontainer.heading: List of UN Regulations as referred to in Article 4(2)

content.p: Estructura preservada — list of 18 Regulations repealed by this Regulation.

conclusions.p: This Regulation shall be binding in its entirety and directly applicable in all Member States.
